title: Segmented QA compression benchmark — long lawful spans carry usable structure; CONSISTENT with QA_CORE_SPEC.v1 lawful-generator organization
_fixture_purpose: PASS fixture: repo-native empirical observation from qa_compression_benchmark.py. The benchmark compared a whole-trace QA codec and a thresholded segmented QA codec against strong lossless baselines (zstd, brotli, xz, bzip2, gzip) on QA-native synthetic trajectories and controls. Result: whole-trace QA remains best on clean lawful cycles, while segmented QA materially reduces the failure mode on corrupted lawful traces without claiming dominance over strong generic codecs. This supports the narrower project claim that lawful generator structure carries real organizational information, but does NOT certify a general QA compression theorem.

observation.summary: On clean QA cycle traces, qa_codec+zstd beats the best generic baseline by 22.03% (92 B vs 118 B). On noisy lawful traces, thresholded segmented QA reduces the prior whole-trace failure from 20729 B to 13129 B at 100k states, narrowing the gap to the best generic baseline xz_9e=12372 B from 67.55% worse to 6.12% worse. On random control data, segmented QA narrows the gap to brotli_11 from 6.35% worse to 0.71% worse. Interpretation: QA compression is effective as a lawful-span encoder layered over a generic backend, not as a replacement for strong general-purpose compression.

parent_cert.claim: lawful generator structure carries genuine information about trajectory organization, so QA state evolution is not arbitrary labelling but reflects real dynamical organization under the chosen generator algebra

evidence.description: Clean lawful cycle remains strongly QA-compressible under the whole-trace law codec
evidence.value: 100k states: qa_codec+zstd=92 B vs best generic baseline brotli_11=118 B (-22.03%)

evidence.description: Thresholded lawful-span segmentation materially reduces the noisy-trace failure mode of whole-trace QA compression
evidence.value: 100k noisy cycle: whole-trace qa_codec+zstd=20729 B; segmented qa_segmented_codec+zstd=13129 B (36.67% improvement)

evidence.description: Segmented QA approaches but does not beat the strongest generic baseline on corrupted lawful traces
evidence.value: 100k noisy cycle: qa_segmented_codec+zstd=13129 B vs xz_9e=12372 B (+6.12%)

evidence.description: On random control data, segmented QA is nearly neutral rather than catastrophically wrong
evidence.value: 100k random control: qa_segmented_codec+zstd=117389 B vs brotli_11=116561 B (+0.71%)

evidence.description: The benchmark supports a narrow interpretation: QA works as a selective lawful-span encoder over a generic backend, not as a general-purpose compression replacement
evidence.value: segmenting long lawful spans preserves the structural win while avoiding the all-or-nothing behavior of whole-trace QA tokenization

open_questions: Does the lawful-span effect survive on real repo-native traces such as cert/event logs, reachability traces, and theorem-engine traces?
Can a global or dynamic-programming segmenter outperform the current fixed-threshold span selection?
How much of the remaining advantage gap on noisy traces is due to container overhead versus missed lawful spans?

validation_checks.description: observation.source is a known source type
verdict is one of {CONSISTENT, CONTRADICTS, PARTIAL, INCONCLUSIVE}
verdict==CONTRADICTS implies nonempty fail_ledger (not applicable here)
